items.summary: The U.S. and Iran on Monday were weighing the framework of a plan to end their five‑week-old conflict, even as Tehran pushed back against pressure to swiftly reopen the Strait of Hormuz on the eve of a new ultimatum set by Trump.

items.headline: 'Regime change' in the Strait of Hormuz sees 20,000 sailors stranded at sea
items.summary: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.headline: Oil prices surge while Asian share prices rise moderately
items.summary: Oil prices spike amid Iran war concerns as Asian markets show modest gains in cautious trading.

items.headline: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
items.summary: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.

items.summary: Russian drone strikes in Odesa claimed civilian lives including a toddler. Ukrainian drones retaliated targeting Russia's oil export ports.  The attacks damaged infrastructure and injured civilians. Ukraine faces ongoing challenges with air defense.  Russia c…

items.headline: Ukraine Hits Russian Oil Facilities, Says At Least 1 Civilian Killed In Russian Strikes
items.summary: Ukraine has kept up its attacks on Russian oil export and production infrastructure, hitting facilities in two regions, while widespread Russian drone attacks on Ukraine killed at least one civilian overnight and fighting persisted at the front, officials sai…

items.headline: US Search For Missing Crew Member Continues, As Strike Reported Near Iran Nuclear Plant
items.summary: Rescue teams are continuing to search for a US crew member missing after their fighter jet was brought down over Iran, as reports emerged of a strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant.

items.headline: Satellite images begin to show damage wrought by Iran war
items.summary: Satellite images are emerging, offering a view of the Iran war's damage. Ships are seen burning in an Iranian port. Military bases in the region show destroyed buildings. Information on the war's toll has been limited. High-resolution images from various firm…

items.headline: IRGC pressure secured Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran's new Supreme Leader, sources confirm
items.summary: Iranian sources reveal Revolutionary Guards forced Mojtaba Khamenei's selection as Supreme Leader, overcoming pragmatist opposition and Assembly of Experts concerns. IRGC threatened critics and used arguments of wartime urgency to secure his appointment by March 8.

dimension_signal.rationale: IRGC pressure tactics securing leadership position demonstrates heavy reliance on military support for political survival

items.headline: New Iran Supreme Leader issues first message via state TV, health questions persist
items.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei's first message as Supreme Leader was read by TV anchor showing only his photograph, raising questions about his condition after reports he was injured in February strikes. Called for continued military resistance and keeping Strait of Hormuz closed.

dimension_signal.rationale: inability to appear publicly and reliance on pre-recorded messages indicates weakened position and health concerns

items.headline: IAEA reports Iran blocks access to nuclear facilities for eight months amid war
items.summary: International Atomic Energy Agency confirms Iran has denied inspectors access to several nuclear facilities since June 2025 strikes, including Isfahan facility storing 400kg of 60% enriched uranium. E3 nations express deep concern over lack of transparency.

items.headline: Pakistan mediates US-Iran ceasefire talks as Field Marshal Munir emerges as key broker
items.summary: Field Marshal Asim Munir positioned as central mediator in US-Iran peace efforts, using Revolutionary Guard contacts to deliver 15-point ceasefire proposal. Pakistan's neutral stance aided by avoiding Iranian targeting during war.

dimension_signal.rationale: positioning as peace mediator between major powers demonstrates pragmatic diplomatic approach over ideological confrontation

items.headline: Xi Jinping purges 100 senior PLA officers including Central Military Commission members
items.summary: By early 2026, Chinese President Xi Jinping detained or removed nearly 100 senior military officers in expanded anti-corruption campaign, including five of six Central Military Commission members. Purge leaves PLA high command in disarray, reducing credibility as Taiwan threat.

dimension_signal.rationale: massive military purges removing his own appointees suggests deep trust issues and weakens military capability against Taiwan

items.headline: Trump approval rating hits 33% on Iran war handling as Congress rejects war powers resolution
items.summary: President Trump's approval for Iran war handling sits at 33%, with overall rating at 35%. Senate rejected war powers resolution 53-47 in March, House followed 219-212. Administration preparing $50 billion supplemental funding request for Iran operations.

dimension_signal.rationale: low approval ratings and congressional resistance to war powers demonstrate reduced domestic political accountability

items.headline: Netanyahu coalition advances bill to cancel fraud charges in ongoing corruption trial
items.summary: Israeli coalition lawmakers announced bill to repeal fraud and breach of trust crimes that Netanyahu faces in three corruption cases. Opposition calls measure a 'coup' as PM also submitted formal pardon request to President Herzog in November.

dimension_signal.rationale: attempting to retroactively cancel criminal charges through legislative action represents direct undermining of judicial accountability

items.headline: ICC Appeals Chamber upholds Netanyahu arrest warrants despite Israeli jurisdiction challenges
items.summary: International Criminal Court Appeals Chamber rejected Israel's admissibility challenge by 3-2 vote in December 2025, keeping arrest warrants against Netanyahu and Gallant active. Two other preliminary motions challenging warrant validity remain pending.

dimension_signal.rationale: ICC's rejection of jurisdiction challenges maintains international legal pressure and accountability mechanisms

items.headline: Xi Jinping deploys PLA Cyberspace Force to South China Sea artificial islands
items.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping's Lunar New Year message revealed PLA Cyberspace Force stationed on Fiery Cross Reef in disputed Spratly Islands conducting 'combat readiness duty'. Rare public appearance of mysterious cyber warfare unit in South China Sea.

dimension_signal.rationale: deployment of cyber warfare capabilities to disputed territories signals increasingly aggressive military posture over territorial claims
